page: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.4 GHz and a 320 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance includes 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput and low-latency data access. Network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth, facilitating rapid data transfer across distributed environments. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place the multi-core processing capabilities of the i7i.metal-48xl in the top tier, with strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier read and write speeds for large uncached blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, executing hundreds of millions of operations per second. Single-core performance and memory latency tests fall into the average range.

Given its massive local NVMe storage capacity, high memory density, and dedicated compute resources, this server is highly cost-efficient for large-scale database deployments, high-performance computing, big data analytics, and intensive ETL processes. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions also supports efficient CPU-based LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8559C processors and 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory. It is equipped with 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, database operations, and memory bandwidth, while single-core performance remains average. With advanced hardware features like Intel AMX and AVX-512, it is highly optimized for parallel processing and cryptographic tasks. The platform's massive storage capacity and high memory bandwidth provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive enterprise applications without requiring external storage attachments. This configuration is best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server featuring massive local NVMe storage, dense DDR5 memory, and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.
